articles.title: Ten Steps Towards Happiness (2015)
articles.summary: Investing in one's senses, learning new skills, enjoying others, and being part of larger communities can lead to increased happiness and well-being for individuals. These steps are not about achieving a specific goal or outcome but rather about cultivating a mindset that values personal growth and connection with others. By focusing on the present moment and engaging with the world around us, we can experience a sense of fulfillment and joy.

articles.title: How an AI Anime Is Created
articles.summary: Creating a custom anime requires a combination of human creativity and AI-powered tools to bring the story to life. While AI can generate rough drafts and character references, it struggles with pacing, emotion, and consistency in animation and post-production. Human writers, directors, and animators work together to refine the script, direct scenes, and edit the final product. The key to creating an engaging anime lies not only in the writing but also in the editing and directing process, which are still largely human-made.

articles.title: Bringing the modern web to the original iPad mini
articles.summary: The original iPad mini was brought back to life by resurrecting iOS 6 with a remote browser, allowing developers to create a smooth and distraction-free reading experience on the device. articles.title: "Drawing" the Mona Lisa with GPT-5.6, Claude, Gemini, and Grok
articles.summary: A team of developers built an arena where they drew the Mona Lisa with different vision models, including GPT-5.6 Sol, Claude Fable 5, Grok 4.5, and Gemini 3.6 Flash, to see how each model performed in a variety of tasks. The goal was to understand whether these models could produce high-quality drawings that met user expectations, and to explore the trade-offs between cost and capability. The results showed that while some models excelled at simple drawing tasks, others struggled with more complex ones, highlighting the importance of considering the specific use case when evaluating model performance.

articles.title: A digestion of the Jacobian conjecture counterexample
articles.summary: The Jacobian conjecture, which states that a polynomial map in complex variables with a non-zero constant Jacobian is invertible, has been proven false in three dimensions by a counterexample. This finding relies on the Lefschetz principle and the Fable AI algorithm to demonstrate that such a polynomial exists. The existence of this counterexample challenges the conjecture's assertion that local invertibility implies global invertibility.

articles.title: FreeInk: Open ecosystem for e-readers
articles.summary: FreeInk is an open-source project that aims to create a more affordable and accessible e-reader ecosystem. The core technology behind FreeInk involves a modular design that allows users to swap out individual components, such as displays and processors, without having to replace the entire device. This approach enables developers to create customized e-readers with unique features and price points. By promoting open innovation, FreeInk could democratize access to e-reading technology, making it more viable for a wider range of users.

articles.title: git --end-of-options
articles.summary: git --end-of-options is a flag that allows specifying options after the end of option parsing in Git commands. This feature was introduced to separate revisions from pathspecs and prevent ambiguous log output. The introduction of this flag has been met with criticism, particularly regarding its potential misuse as an attack vector. The development of git rev-parse only as a subcommand and the addition of --end-of-options as a standalone flag have raised concerns about argument injection vulnerabilities.

articles.title: Thoughts On Integers (2023)
articles.summary: Thoughts On Integers in C and its implications for developers are centered around the traditional integer types available in systems programming languages such as C. These types include int, char, short, long, and long long, each with distinct sizes and uses. The article highlights how these integer types behave when signed or unsigned integers overflow, with signed integers invoking undefined behavior and unsigned integers wrapping on overflow. It also notes that some modern languages like Go, Swift, and Rust have simplified the use of int by allowing explicit sizing and defaulting to panicking in cases where unsigned integers are used.
